meta.title: Map the path before the deadline maps you.
meta.subtitle: Use this board to compare routes, protect backup plans, and keep Wake Tech in play as a launch point toward larger goals in North Carolina.
meta.notice: All admissions and transfer rules should be verified against current official sources before making a decision.

stats.label: best time to start organizing source-backed facts

paths.title: Stay active on waitlists
paths.description: If a student is still alive at a target school, keep the file moving while backup paths are built in parallel.
actions: Track deadlines for updates, final grades, and interest signals.
Collect any new academic evidence that strengthens the file.
Treat the waitlist as real, but never as the only plan.

paths.title: Use Wake Tech as a proving ground
paths.description: A focused first year or first two years at Wake Tech can build a stronger transfer case and create leverage through completed college work.
actions: Choose classes that transfer cleanly and support the intended major.
Protect GPA and course rigor from the first term forward.
Track school-specific transfer requirements instead of assuming a generic path.

paths.title: Build options wider than two schools
paths.description: A plan is stronger when it includes target schools, strong backups, and a route that still works if one decision goes sideways.
actions: Keep a short list of additional North Carolina universities that fit the student's goals.
Separate emotional favorites from practical fallback choices.
Use deadlines, cost, fit, and transfer friendliness as filters.

destinations.name: NC State
destinations.focus: Strong for majors where sequencing and major readiness matter early.
destinations.summary: The planning lens here is not just admission, but whether the intended major has special prerequisites, GPA pressure, or limited room for mistakes.
whatToTrack: transfer application windows and required materials
credit articulation for Wake Tech classes

destinations.name: UNC-Chapel Hill
destinations.focus: Holistic review still matters, but transfer planning must stay evidence-driven.
destinations.summary: For UNC, the strongest route is usually the one that combines clean academics, deliberate course choices, and a clear personal narrative backed by performance.
whatToTrack: required transfer coursework and credit expectations
waitlist movement and update rules for the current cycle
programs or pathways that improve eventual transfer odds

roadmap.title: Protect every viable option
items: keep live applications and waitlists current
gather all transcript, score, and grade data into one place
start source-backed research instead of relying on memory

roadmap.title: Translate uncertainty into a concrete plan
items: identify the most likely majors or academic lanes
map those lanes against Wake Tech coursework and university requirements
build a primary plan plus at least one backup

roadmap.title: Turn planning into execution
items: register for the right courses early
maintain a high college GPA from day one
update the dashboard as official information is verified

checklists.title: Decision Quality
items: backup options that are actually acceptable

questions: Which Wake Tech classes create the cleanest path into the most likely target major?
What rules apply differently to first-year applicants, waitlisted students, and transfer applicants?
Which deadlines create true points of no return this year?
What additional schools should stay in the mix if NCSU or UNC do not open up?

glossary.definition: The way a class at one school is recognized for credit at another school.
How prepared a student is for a specific program based on required courses already completed.
College credit earned before enrolling at the destination university.
The set of actions used to stay viable with a school while building other plans at the same time.
